title: Slovakian Wild Garlic Leaves Dumplings
description: A sweet and light Slovakian dish featuring wild garlic leaves, perfect for a midnight craving, made with halal-friendly ingredients and mushimono-steam cooking method.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine flour, eggs, milk, melted butter, salt, and sugar, and mix until a smooth dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes elastic.
Divide the dough into small balls, about the size of a small egg.
Roll out each ball into a thin circle.
Place a few wild garlic leaves in the center of each circle.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half-moon shape, and press the edges together to seal the dumpling.
Repeat with the remaining dough and filling.
In a large steamer basket, arrange the dumplings in a single layer, leaving some space between each dumpling.
Steam the dumplings over boiling water for 10 minutes, or until they are cooked through and fluffy.
Serve the dumplings hot, garnished with additional wild garlic leaves if desired.
